---
title: "3.8 Scope and Access"
subtitle: "Class Creation"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit3-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### Scope decides where a name can be used

<span class="term">Scope</span> (作用域) is the region of code where a variable or method name can be accessed.

This topic covers:

::: {.tight-list}
- class-level scope
- method-level scope
- block-level scope
- local variables
- access modifiers
:::

## Scope Diagram

### Different levels have different lifetimes

![Scope diagram](../../assets/3-class-creation/3-8-scope-and-access/scopeDiagram.png){fig-align="center" width="50%"}

Instance variables are declared in the class but outside constructors and methods. Local variables are declared inside methods or blocks.

## Class-Level Scope

### Instance variables can be used by instance methods

```java
public class Person
{
    private String name;

    public void printName()
    {
        System.out.println(name);
    }
}
```

`name` has class-level scope within the object and can be used by instance methods.

## Method-Level Scope

### Parameters and locals live inside one method

```java
public void setName(String newName)
{
    String trimmed = newName.trim();
    name = trimmed;
}
```

`newName` and `trimmed` can be used only inside `setName`.

## Block-Level Scope

### Variables inside braces stay inside braces

```java
if (score >= 90)
{
    String letter = "A";
    System.out.println(letter);
}

// letter cannot be used here
```

The variable `letter` exists only inside the `if` block.

## Local Variable Error

### Do not use a local outside its scope

Problem:

```java
public void printTwice()
{
    String message = "Hi";
}

public void printLater()
{
    System.out.println(message);
}
```

`message` is local to `printTwice`; `printLater` cannot access it.

## Access Modifiers

### Access is different from scope

Scope is where a name exists. Access is who is allowed to use it.

::: {.compare-grid}
::: {.soft-box}
**`private`**

- accessible only inside the class
- used for instance variables
:::
::: {.soft-box}
**`public`**

- accessible from other classes
- used for constructors and intended methods
:::
:::

## Groupwork Coding Challenge

### Debugging scope

Fix the class by moving variables to the right scope or passing values as parameters.

```java
public class ScopeBug
{
    public void methodOne()
    {
        int count = 5;
    }

    public void methodTwo()
    {
        System.out.println(count);
    }
}
```

One fix is to make `count` an instance variable if both methods need it.

## Classroom Check

### A strong answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- distinguish class-level, method-level, and block-level scope
- identify instance variables versus local variables
- explain why a variable declared in one method cannot be used in another method
- distinguish scope from access modifiers
- debug a variable access problem by moving the declaration or passing a parameter
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
